data.title: Best Buy jumps amid speculation it could be target for GameStop
data.description:
Best Buy (BBY) rose 5.3% amid speculation that the electronics retailer may be a potential takeover target for GameStop (GME).

GameStop (GME) Chairman and CEO Ryan Cohen said in late January he aims to pull off a "very, very, very big" acquisition of a larger consumer company that would be transformational for its future.

It appears that GameStop (GME) bought a position on swap and this is likely the target that Cohen is circling, Gordon Haskett's Don Bilson wrote in a note on Thursday.  Bilson didn't speculate on which company GameStop may be targeting. 

GameStop disclosed in its latest 10-K filing that it “posted approximately $0.7 billion of cash into an account of the Company that is pledged as collateral for certain existing and potential cash or physically settled derivative transactions.”

Bilson said previously he speculated that Best Buy (BBY) could be a target and there was some prime broker action in BBY in Q4, but that doesn't "square with" GameStop (GME)  saying it put its money to work after the fiscal year ended. 

GameStop didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment. Shares of GameStop fell 2.3%.

data.title: Kansas City Fed Composite Index jumps to 11 in March
data.description:
The Kansas City Composite Index came in at 11 in March, compared to 5 in February, according to data from the district Federal Reserve bank released on Thursday.

The composite index is an average of the production, new orders, employment, supplier delivery time, and raw materials inventory indexes.

The Manufacturing Index also stood at 11 in March, compared to 10 prior.

Both durable and nondurable manufacturing activity increased in March. Growth in the durable manufacturing sector was driven primarily by wood product manufacturing, while growth in the nondurable manufacturing sector was driven by paper and plastics and rubber product manufacturing. 

The month-over-month indexes were all positive except for new orders for exports.

The volume of shipments and new orders indexes increased, with readings of 20 and 15, respectively. The employment index rebounded into positive territory from -7 to 6.

Expectations for future activity grew further with the composite index increasing from 15 to 16, as expectations for production ticked up.

data.title: Expand Energy is the worst performing large-cap energy stock YTD
data.description:
U.S. large-cap energy stocks have been standout performers year-to-date, with the S&P 500 Energy sector up roughly 14%–15% in 2026, making it the best-performing group in the index as higher crude prices and supply risks tied to Iran and Venezuela support momentum.

In light of this, below is a list of the 10 worst-performing large-cap energy stocks ranked by their year-to-date performance. The list focuses on companies in the oil and gas sector, including those in exploration and production as well as storage and transportation.

The list is topped by Expand Energy Corporation (EXE), with a YTD performance of just 3.23%. Western Midstream Partners, LP (WES) and MPLX LP (MPLX) follow, with DT Midstream, Inc. (DTM) and Energy Transfer LP (ET) rounding out the bottom five. Most of these companies operate in the Oil and Gas Storage and Transportation industry, with a couple in Oil and Gas Exploration and Production.

While the majority of stocks on this list carry a Hold rating, DT Midstream, Inc. (DTM) stands out with a Sell rating and a quant score of 2.46. Other notable names include Enterprise Products Partners L.P. (EPD), The Williams Companies, Inc. (WMB), and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (KMI), all of which maintain Hold ratings despite their relatively modest YTD gains.

Here is the list:

  1. Expand Energy Corporation (EXE), YTD perf: 3.23%, Quant rating: Hold 3.08

  2. Western Midstream Partners, LP Common Units (WES), YTD perf: 6.35%, Quant rating: Hold 3.04

  3. MPLX LP Common Units (MPLX), YTD perf: 10.34%, Quant rating: Hold 3.21

  4. DT Midstream, Inc. (DTM), YTD perf: 13.76%, Quant rating: Sell 2.46

  5. Energy Transfer LP Common Units (ET), YTD perf: 16.07%, Quant rating: Hold 3.34

  6. Enterprise Products Partners L.P. Common Units (EPD), YTD perf: 21.65%, Quant rating: Hold 3.24

  7. The Williams Companies, Inc. (WMB), YTD perf: 22.79%, Quant rating: Hold 3.21

  8. Viper Energy, Inc. (VNOM), YTD perf: 22.86%, Quant rating: Hold 3.19

  9. Plains All American Pipeline, L.P. Common Units (PAA), YTD perf: 23.33%, Quant rating: Hold 3.29

  10. Kinder Morgan, Inc. (KMI), YTD perf: 23.61%, Quant rating: Hold 3.27

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC)

data.title: Google's TurboQuant leads to more intense computing rather than dimming demand: Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Google's (GOOG)(GOOGL) TurboQuant, a compression algorithm that optimally addresses the challenge of memory overhead in vector quantization, will likely lead to the usage of more intensive AI applications rather than diluting demand for memory and storage products, according to Morgan Stanley.

"It is being widely reported that Google has reduced memory usage by 6x, which leaves out that they are just talking about KV Cache memory, not memory overall," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Joseph Moore, in a Thursday investor report. "Memory stocks sold off again, at least partly due to this hyperbole."

"It's also worth noting that Google's Gemini 3 and 2.5 Pro models feature a 1M token context window, but the company has disclosed that they tested up to 10M tokens with great results with Gemini 1.5 Pro but chose not to release the model in part due to inference costs," Moore said. "So we would expect that as costs come down due to innovations like these and others, it will be used to serve higher intelligence, more compute-intensive products."

In response, Morgan Stanley reiterated its Overweight rating on Micron Technology (MU) and Sandisk (SNDK). The analysts point out that limited memory availability is the primary constraint on AI demand. This is particularly true with DRAM, as data center demand on memory is affecting the production of PCs and smartphones.

"There's just no indication that demand for memory or storage is going down, universally across our contacts," Moore added.

data.title: Noodles rips a +50% gain off improved sales, margin results
data.description:
Noodles & Company (NDLS) ripped a 55% gain in early trading on Thursday after topping estimates with its Q4 earnings report. Revenue was only up 0.8% off a smaller store base, but comparable restaurant sales increased 6.6% system-wide.

Restaurant contribution margin in the quarter increased to 14.1% from 11.2%, driven by a combination of menu price, vendor rebates, and lower discounting, partially offset by higher food costs associated with our new menu offerings and modest inflation.

Noodles (NDLS) management said the chain has built meaningful and sustained momentum, with system-wide comparable sales growth escalating to over 9% in the current quarter. "In the first quarter thus far, we have delivered continued increases in traffic and same-store sales with system-wide comparable sales growth over 9% and traffic over 4%. March will mark our seventh consecutive period of traffic growth, and notably, period 2 of 2026 delivered one of the strongest comparable sales performances in the company's 31-year history," highlighted CEO Joseph Christina.

Shares of Noodles (NDLS) traded as high as $9.61 earlier in the session to threaten the 52-week high of $10.00.

data.title: United Natural Foods a unique way "to play the healthy living trend"—Wells Fargo
data.description:
Shares of United Natural Foods (UNFI) set another record high on Thursday as progress on its turnaround efforts, Amazon (AMZN) relationship, and “beatable” FY28 targets led Wells Fargo to view the stock as a buy with an upgrade to Overweight from Equal Weight and a 40% hike to the firm’s target price.

Analyst Edward Kelly lays out his argument for growth in UNFI’s natural and organic position, which gives investors a unique way to “play the healthy living trend.”

UNFI also stands to see meaningful efficiency gains from AI integration “as the scaled player in a logistics-heavy business with a large warehouse network, private fleet, and high cost of labor,” Kelly says. With already low margins, AI efficiencies will magnify even small wins, with every 5 basis points of margin expansion adding 230 basis points to EBITDA growth.

“Deleverage efforts are on track, upside to 3-year targets through 2028 looks increasingly likely, and shares are simply too cheap with a double-digit percentage yield,” Kelly adds.

While most Wall Street analysts remain on the sidelines, Seeking Alpha authors mostly view United Natural Foods as a Buy, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating gives the stock a Strong Buy endorsement with a near-perfect Quant score of 4.96. 

From the same time last year, UNFI has appreciated by more than 70%, outperforming the S&P 500 by 55 percentage points.

data.title: Rockwell Medical targets $1M–$2M adjusted EBITDA and positive cash flow in 2026 as product mix diversifies
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Rockwell Medical (RMTI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • President, CEO & Director Mark Strobeck highlighted that 2025 was a defining year, stating, “We successfully navigated changes in our customer base, changes in our customer purchasing volumes and changes in our distribution footprint all while maintaining profitability on an adjusted EBITDA basis for the second consecutive year.” He emphasized significant operational changes, alignment of infrastructure with demand, and delivering one of the highest quarterly gross margins in company history. Strobeck also noted positive cash flow from operations in the fourth quarter and a higher year-end cash position.

  • Strobeck detailed progress in diversifying the customer base, noting Rockwell now serves approximately 300 customers in the U.S., including all five leading dialysis providers, and supplies to over 30 countries. He cited key agreements: continued supply to Fresenius with growth expected in 2026, an extended agreement with DaVita through the end of 2026 with increased pricing, a multiyear deal with Innovative Renal Care now covering 70% of their clinics, and a new agreement with Concerto Renal Services covering 100% of their facilities. He also highlighted the rapid onboarding of 30 new West Coast customers following a competitor’s supply chain exit.

  • The company further diversified its product portfolio, introducing a single-use bicarbonate cartridge, FDA 510(k) approved, with sales expected to reach approximately $1 million in 2026. Strobeck described structural operational improvements, stating, “Our margin expansion, especially in the fourth quarter of 2025 is not the result of temporary actions or onetime benefits. Instead, these changes reflect structural improvements in how we run our business.”

  • The appointment of Rashad Brown as Vice President of Manufacturing and Supply Chain was highlighted, with Strobeck emphasizing anticipated further improvements in manufacturing efficiencies.

  • Strobeck projected, “For our 2026 guidance, we believe we are well positioned to advance our strategy to drive sustainable revenue growth, expand our profitability and further diversify our portfolio. As a result, we project our business operations in 2026 will generate adjusted EBITDA between $1 million and $2 million and operating cash flow to be positive.”

  • CFO Jesse Neri stated, “We increased our cash position from $17.3 million at the end of March 2025 to $25 million by the end of the year. Gross margin grew from 16% in Q1 to 21% in Q4. And adjusted EBITDA improved each quarter, starting at negative $400,000 in Q1 of '25 and ended with a positive $1 million in Q4.”

Outlook

  • Strobeck stated that the company expects business operations in 2026 to generate adjusted EBITDA between $1 million and $2 million and positive operating cash flow. He noted, “Because we are currently in negotiations with several large customers, the outcome of which has the potential to positively impact both net sales and gross margin in 2026, we expect to provide guidance on those financial metrics in the near future.”

  • Longer-term goals include reaching annual net sales above $100 million by 2029, gross margins approaching 30%, and annual profitability in the $5 million to $10 million range, with the caveat that these are directional views subject to risk.

Financial Results

  • CFO Neri reported net sales for Q4 2025 of $18.3 million, up 15% from Q3 2025, but a 26% decrease from Q4 2024. Full year 2025 net sales were $69.3 million, down 32% year-over-year.

  • Gross profit for Q4 2025 was $3.9 million, a 70% increase over Q3 2025, and in line with Q4 2024. Gross margin in Q4 2025 was 21%, higher than 14% in Q3 and 15% in Q4 2024. Net loss for Q4 2025 was $600,000, an improvement over $1.8 million in Q3.

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2025 was $1 million, up from $50,000 in Q3. Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $300,000. Cash at year-end 2025 was $25 million, up from $23.7 million at the end of Q3.

Q&A

  • Anthony Vendetti, Maxim Group: Asked about DaVita’s 2026 volume commitments and West Coast expansion. Strobeck responded that DaVita is purchasing at or above projected volumes and said, “We are hopeful that there is an opportunity here, not only in establishing a much longer-term relationship, but also the possibility of securing additional business with them.” On West Coast expansion, Strobeck described the company’s commercial strategy and partnership with B.Braun to target new dialysis centers. He estimated the at-home dialysis market is trending toward 10% of the overall market, with Rockwell positioned for growth as demand increases.

  • Raghuram Selvaraju, H.C. Wainwright: Asked about DaVita’s contribution to long-term projections and the competitive landscape. CFO Neri said the company assumes consistent DaVita volumes in projections. Strobeck explained the competitive landscape is dominated by three companies and asserted Rockwell’s competitive advantage is “high-quality products” and “cost-effective” transportation. He also described Western expansion as a multimillion-dollar opportunity and discussed ongoing business development for higher-margin products.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on sustainability of key customer relationships, growth in new markets, and profitability, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone, with questions probing for specifics on contracts, margins, and expansion.

  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined tone, emphasizing structural improvements, operational discipline, and long-term growth outlook. Strobeck stated, “We are pleased to report for the third year in a row, our annual performance was aligned with our annual guidance.”

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management exhibited increased confidence, with management delivering more specific forward-looking targets and analysts seeking greater clarity on execution and customer commitments.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from a general expectation of early-year visibility (Q3) to a specific projection for 2026 adjusted EBITDA and cash flow (Q4).

  • Management’s tone in Q4 was more confident, providing concrete forward projections and highlighting operational improvements as structural rather than temporary.

  • Key metrics improved quarter-over-quarter: Q4 net sales rose to $18.3 million from $15.9 million in Q3, gross margin increased to 21% from 14%, and adjusted EBITDA rose to $1 million from $50,000. Net loss narrowed materially. Cash position improved.

  • Analysts in Q4 focused more on growth prospects with major accounts, competitive dynamics, and expansion, compared to Q3’s questions about transition management and contract finalization.

Risks and Concerns

  • Customer concentration risk was cited as a challenge, with management highlighting efforts to diversify the customer base and secure multiyear contracts.

  • Supply chain disruptions were addressed through rapid production scaling and logistics expansion, especially in the western U.S.

  • Management discussed ongoing negotiations with large customers as a source of potential volatility in future guidance.

  • Analysts voiced concerns about the durability of major customer agreements (e.g., DaVita) and the ability to sustain margin improvements.

Final Takeaway

Management underscored the success of Rockwell Medical’s operational realignment, improved gross margin, and the expansion of key customer relationships in 2025. Looking to 2026, the company projects adjusted EBITDA between $1 million and $2 million, positive operating cash flow, and continued product diversification, with longer-term goals including annual net sales above $100 million and gross margins approaching 30%. Customer mix diversification and manufacturing optimization are expected to support sustainable profitability and growth in the years ahead.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
